title: Coordinating conjunctions
description: Identify coordinating conjunctions and their functions.
topic_path: Parts of speech: the preposition and the conjunction

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>We expected to see a rabbit behind the magician’s satin cape; \_\_\_\_\_, when he removed it, the bunny was gone!

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that introduces the unexpected occurrence in the second clause.

The best choice is **"yet"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>Liz didn’t want to go grocery shopping, \_\_\_\_\_\_ did she want to stop at the dry cleaner’s.


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that connects two things that Liz *doesn't* want to do.

The best choice is **"nor"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>Desteni loves being outside, \_\_\_\_\_\_ she walks home from school every day.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that Desteni walks home *because* she loves to be outside.

The best choice is **"so"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>Mr. Winston was very full, \_\_\_\_\_\_ he had eaten an entire pot roast.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that Winston is full *because* he ate an entire pot roast.

The best choice is **"for"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>I wanted to remember this day at the beach forever, \_\_\_\_\_\_ I took lots of pictures.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that taking pictures is a *consequence* of wanting to remember the day.

The best choice is **"so"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>I wanted to become a surgeon, \_\_\_\_\_\_ I went to medical school.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that going to med school is a *consequence* of wanting to be a surgeon.

The best choice is **"so"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>I have to get a flu shot, \_\_\_\_\_\_ I don’t want to.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that introduces a conflict with the fact that I need a flu shot.

The best choice is **"but"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>I was confident about my submarine driving exam results, \_\_\_\_\_\_ I had studied.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that I was confident *because* I studied.

The best choice is **"for"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>I had practiced the violin solo for hours,   \_\_\_\_\_\_  I was nervous when I walked on stage.



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that I was nervous *despite the fact* that I'd practiced.

The best choice is **"yet"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>I was surprised when I heard a knock at the door,    \_\_\_\_\_\_   it was very late.



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that I was surprised *because* it was late.

The best choice is **"for"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>Our class went on a trip to the botanical gardens,   \_\_\_\_\_\_   I was too sick to go.



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that I was *an exception* to the class going on a trip.

The best choice is **"but"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>Naomi is afraid of small spaces,    \_\_\_\_\_\_   she always takes the stairs instead of the elevator.



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that shows that taking the stairs is a *consequence* of being afraid of small spaces.

The best choice is **"so"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the conjunction that fits best in this sentence.**

>The movie is starting in five minutes,    \_\_\_\_\_\_   we haven’t even bought popcorn yet!



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Conjunctions connect ideas within a sentence and show how those ideas are related.

You can remember conjunctions using the acronym **FANBOYS**:

- **For**: “since, because”
- **And**: combines one thing with another thing
- **Nor**: expresses a negative/that something is untrue
- **But**: expresses exceptions
- **Or**: chooses between options
- **Yet**: expresses the contrary of what would be expected
- **So**: shows the effect or consequences of something.

We need a conjunction that *connects* two ideas that both express urgency about getting to the movie.

The best choice is **"and"**.
